title: Expressing support for the designation of the week of May 4, 2025, through May 10, 2025, as "Tardive Dyskinesia Awareness Week".
plain_summary: This bill asks Congress to officially support making the week of May 4-10, 2025 into Tardive Dyskinesia Awareness Week. Tardive dyskinesia is a disorder that causes people to make movements they cannot control. It can happen to people who take certain medicines for mental health problems or stomach issues. The bill wants Americans to learn more about this condition during that special week.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution asks Congress to support making the week of May fourth through May tenth, two thousand twenty-five, into Tardive Dyskinesia Awareness Week.

sections.title: Background Findings
sections.text: Many people with serious mental health problems like bipolar disorder, major depression, schizophrenia, and schizoaffective disorder need medications to help them. People with stomach problems like gastroparesis, upset stomach, nausea, and vomiting also need certain medications. These medications are called dopamine receptor blocking agents, including antipsychotics and antiemetics. While these medications are necessary for ongoing treatment, using them for a long time may cause tardive dyskinesia. Tardive dyskinesia is a movement disorder where people cannot control abnormal and repetitive movements of their face, body, arms, legs, fingers, or toes. Even mild symptoms of tardive dyskinesia can hurt people physically, socially, and emotionally. About six hundred thousand people in the United States have tardive dyskinesia. About sixty-five percent of people with tardive dyskinesia have not been diagnosed, which makes it important to teach people about the symptoms. People taking these medications should be watched for tardive dyskinesia by a healthcare provider. The American Psychiatric Association recommends regular screening for tardive dyskinesia. Clinical research has led to treatments for adults with tardive dyskinesia that the Food and Drug Administration has approved. Finding and treating tardive dyskinesia can help many people who have psychotic and mood disorders. The House of Representatives can help teach the public and medical community about tardive dyskinesia.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives expresses support for designating Tardive Dyskinesia Awareness Week. The House also encourages every person in the United States to learn more about and become aware of tardive dyskinesia.
